text: Abstract The rapid development of deep learning (DL) has made the corresponding theories and technologies successfully applied in practice a brand-new decision-making method based on evidence. The characteristic function of DL is the representation learning based on non-linear deep structure of the data, which can achieve strong processing ability through the deep-level non-linear transformation of the data feature at least two times. Although deep learning is still an infant scientific field in the form of interdisciplinary research, the survey papers on deep learning model are few that covers all related works. This paper is aimed at summarizing the development of several new models of DL and also putting forward their corresponding applications and corresponding advantages and disadvantages. The results are: a large number of deep learning models are with high efficiency in the specific field and can be applied by multiple models, while DL training models have such disadvantages as long training time, high cost and big samples needed in order to achieve better test results. In addition, the models are prone to deception, incorrect classification and also get easily into local minima, which needs to be optimized in order to get more robust models. And meanwhile, DL has already made great achievements in the fields of health care, education, security, business and more. The commonly used models are CNN, GAN, RNN and other recursive neural networks and also autoencoders, while the other models have not yet been applied sufficiently. It should be said that hybrid conventional DL models can also solve the problems that the traditional model has failed to cope with, such as the capsule network that is expected to become the main model for DL in the future. Finally, the paper is aimed at providing necessary information for the stakeholders related to design and use of the deep learning models in the era of big data.
